user_goals_summary: This user writes academic prose that reads as argument rather than documentation. They prioritize logical dependency between sections, motivated design justifications, and precise terminology, and they value strategic moments of plain-language clarity that signal confidence and mark the conceptual core for the reader.

rubric.name: Argumentative Flow
rubric.description: Each paragraph and subsection earns its place by connecting to what came before it through logical dependency, not mere sequence. The reader should feel walked through an argument, not handed a manual.

dimensions.label: Transitions between paragraphs and sections express logical dependency (because, therefore, this requires) rather than mere sequence (next, additionally, also)
Each paragraph's opening connects explicitly to the preceding paragraph's conclusion, claim, or identified gap
The reader can articulate why each section must follow the previous one, not just that it does
Cross-references between sections reinforce the logical architecture rather than serving as mere navigation aids

rubric.name: Design Motivation
rubric.description: Methodological choices are justified before they are described. The reader encounters the problem, failure mode, or gap that necessitates a component before learning what that component does.

dimensions.label: Each major methodological component is preceded by the problem or failure mode it addresses
Naive or alternative approaches are named explicitly to clarify why the chosen approach is necessary
Justifications are specific to the design's internal logic, not generic appeals to best practice or convention
Later components are motivated by the outputs, limitations, or requirements established by earlier components

rubric.name: Strategic Register Variation
rubric.description: The prose is not written at a single uniform register throughout. At structurally pivotal moments — where the conceptual core of a section lives — the author steps back and states the key insight in plain, direct language that demonstrates confident understanding.

dimensions.label: Key conceptual insights are stated in plain, direct language that contrasts with the surrounding formal register
The most important ideas receive rhetorical emphasis through simplicity, not additional complexity
Register shifts occur at structurally pivotal moments (major transitions, framework introductions, conceptual cores)
Plain-language moments convey authority and mastery rather than informality or imprecision

rubric.name: Terminological Precision and Consistency
rubric.description: Key constructs are formally defined and then used with strict consistency. The same concept is referred to with the same term throughout — no unnecessary synonymic variation — and technical terms are clearly distinguished from everyday usage.

dimensions.label: Key constructs are formally defined at their first appearance
The same concept is referred to with the same term throughout the text without unnecessary variation
Technical terms are clearly distinguished from everyday language through formatting, definition, or explicit framing
Terms introduced in one section are reused consistently when the same construct reappears later

rubric.name: Formal Rigor and Specificity
rubric.description: Procedures, protocols, and evaluative criteria are described with enough detail to be reproducible and assessable. Claims about methodological properties are operationalized rather than merely asserted.

dimensions.label: Procedures and protocols are described with sufficient detail that another researcher could replicate them
Claims about methodological properties (validity, reliability, rigor) are operationalized with specific criteria, not just asserted
Statistical or evaluative criteria include precise specifications (thresholds, metrics, significance levels)
The prose maintains the formality and precision expected in peer-reviewed research throughout non-pivot passages

coaching_notes: This user thinks about prose architecturally — they diagnose problems at the level of rhetorical structure and argumentative logic, not at the sentence or grammar level. Their non-negotiable is that methodology reads as motivated argument, not procedural documentation: every component must answer 'why this and not something simpler?' before answering 'how does this work?' They are comfortable with length and complexity but resist uniformity of register, viewing strategic plainness at key moments as a marker of intellectual confidence rather than a concession to accessibility.

rubric.name: Terminological Precision and Consistency
rubric.description: Key constructs are formally defined and then used with strict consistency. The same concept is referred to with the same term throughout — no unnecessary synonymic variation — and technical terms are clearly distinguished from everyday usage.

They value rigorous formal structure and precise terminology as non-negotiable baselines, but invest most revision energy in ensuring logical dependency between paragraphs, motivating design choices before describing them, strategically deploying plain-language pivots at conceptual cores, and making the stakes of findings explicit.

rubric.name: Argumentative Flow
rubric.description: Each paragraph and subsection earns its place through logical dependency, not mere sequence. The section builds toward its most important claim, so the reader senses a destination and experiences individual findings or descriptions as steps in a developing argument rather than items in a catalogue.

dimensions.label: Transitions between paragraphs express logical dependency (because, therefore, this requires) rather than mere sequence (next, additionally, also)
Each paragraph's opening connects explicitly to the preceding paragraph's conclusion, claim, or identified gap
The reader can articulate why each section must follow the previous one, not just that it does
The section builds toward its most important claim, with the reader able to sense the destination early rather than encountering it as a surprise
Individual findings, descriptions, or components function as steps in an argument rather than items in a list or checkboxes to clear

rubric.name: Strategic Register Variation
rubric.description: The prose is not written at a single uniform register throughout. At structurally pivotal moments — where the conceptual core of a section lives — the author steps back and states the key insight in plain, direct language that demonstrates confident mastery. These moments are used sparingly and earn their place.

dimensions.label: Key conceptual insights are stated in plain, direct language that contrasts with the surrounding formal register
The most important ideas receive rhetorical emphasis through simplicity, not additional complexity
Register shifts occur at structurally pivotal moments (major transitions, framework introductions, conceptual cores) rather than at arbitrary points
Plain-language moments convey authority and mastery — demonstrating the author understands the idea well enough to say it simply
Plain-language pivots are used sparingly (once or twice per section) so that each one carries weight

rubric.name: Design Motivation and Justification
rubric.description: Methodological choices, analytical approaches, and evaluative frameworks are justified before they are described. The reader encounters the problem, failure mode, or question that necessitates a component before learning what that component does or what it found.

dimensions.label: Each major component is preceded by the problem, failure mode, or question it addresses
Naive or alternative approaches are named explicitly to clarify why the chosen approach is necessary
Justifications are specific to the design's internal logic, not generic appeals to best practice or convention
Later components are motivated by the outputs, limitations, or requirements established by earlier components
The analytical approach or testing strategy is briefly established before findings are presented

rubric.name: Stakes and Implications
rubric.description: The text articulates what findings and design choices actually mean — not just what was found, but what it buys us, what it changes, and why the reader should care. Key results are accompanied by explicit statements of significance, and major conclusions reframe earlier claims to reveal their shifted meaning.

dimensions.label: Key findings or design choices are accompanied by explicit statements of what they mean or imply
At least one moment draws out practical or theoretical consequences beyond the immediate result
Major findings reframe earlier results, showing how their significance has shifted in light of the new evidence
The reader can identify which findings or claims the author considers most consequential and why

rubric.name: Terminological Precision and Consistency
rubric.description: Key constructs are formally defined and then used with strict consistency. The same concept is referred to with the same term throughout — no unnecessary synonymic variation — and technical terms are clearly distinguished from everyday usage.

dimensions.label: Key constructs are formally defined at their first appearance
The same concept is referred to with the same term throughout the text without unnecessary variation
Technical terms are clearly distinguished from everyday language through formatting, definition, or explicit framing
Terms introduced in one section are reused consistently when the same construct reappears later

rubric.name: Formal Rigor and Specificity
rubric.description: Procedures, protocols, and evaluative criteria are described with enough detail to be reproducible and assessable. Claims about methodological properties are operationalized rather than merely asserted, and statistical reporting is clean and precise.

dimensions.label: Procedures and protocols are described with sufficient detail that another researcher could replicate them
Claims about methodological properties (validity, reliability, rigor) are operationalized with specific criteria, not just asserted
Statistical or evaluative criteria include precise specifications (thresholds, metrics, significance levels)
The prose maintains the formality and precision expected in peer-reviewed research throughout non-pivot passages

coaching_notes: This user's non-negotiables are terminological precision and formal rigor — they expect these as baseline and will not revisit them once satisfied. Their revision energy concentrates overwhelmingly on structural argumentation: every paragraph must earn its place through logical dependency, and every section must build toward its most important claim rather than cataloguing findings. They respond strongly to the 'failure mode' pattern — motivating each component by naming what goes wrong without it. They value plain-language pivots used sparingly at conceptual cores as a signal of authorial confidence, and consistently request that key findings be accompanied by explicit statements of what they buy us practically or theoretically.

coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: every section must read as an argument with logical dependency between components, never as a procedural catalogue or report. The user's revision pattern is consistent — structural and motivational concerns are addressed first, then register and implications are layered in last. Plain-language pivots are highly valued but only when earned; the user explicitly notices and praises the right one ('it multiplies the capacity that is already there') and would reject gratuitous informality. When presenting results, always establish what question is being answered and how before delivering the numbers.
